title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 6: Numbers and Shapes
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Mia bought 3 notebooks for 4 dollars each and a pen for 5 dollars. How much did she spend in total?
questions.solution: 3 notebooks cost 3 x 4 = 12 dollars. Adding the pen: 12 + 5 = 17 dollars.

questions.question_text: How many small unit squares make up the L-shape shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shape made up of unit squares arranged in three rows.
questions.solution: Counting the unit squares: 3 in the top row, 2 in the middle row, 2 in the bottom row. Total = 7.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:15 pm and lasts 1 hour and 30 minutes. At what time does it end?
questions.solution: 3:15 pm + 1 hour = 4:15 pm. Then 4:15 + 30 minutes = 4:45 pm.

questions.question_text: What is the next number in the pattern: 2, 5, 8, 11, __?
questions.solution: Each number increases by 3. So 11 + 3 = 14.

questions.question_text: A large triangle has one vertical line from its top vertex to the base. How many triangles of any size are in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 10,90 110,90'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='90'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle split by a vertical line from apex to base into two smaller triangles.
questions.solution: Two small triangles plus the original big triangle equals 3 triangles in total.

questions.question_text: Leo has 24 stickers. He gives half to his sister. How many stickers does Leo have left?
questions.solution: Half of 24 is 12. Leo keeps 12 stickers.

questions.question_text: A ribbon is 80 cm long. Sara cuts off a piece that is 35 cm long. How long is the remaining ribbon?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='160' height='10'/><line x1='80' y1='5' x2='80' y2='35' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A horizontal ribbon with a dashed line showing a cut.
questions.solution: 80 - 35 = 45 cm remains.

questions.question_text: Anna is taller than Beth. Beth is taller than Carla. Among these three, who is the shortest?
questions.solution: Anna > Beth > Carla in height, so Carla is the shortest.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 18. What is the largest of the three numbers?
questions.solution: The numbers are 5, 6, 7 with sum 18. The largest is 7.

questions.question_text: A rectangle is divided into 5 equal vertical strips. The 1st, 3rd, and 5th strips are shaded. How many strips are NOT shaded?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='28' height='60' fill='lightgray'/><rect x='38' y='10' width='28' height='60' fill='none'/><rect x='66' y='10' width='28' height='60' fill='lightgray'/><rect x='94' y='10' width='28' height='60' fill='none'/><rect x='122' y='10' width='28' height='60' fill='lightgray'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided into 5 vertical strips with the 1st, 3rd, and 5th shaded.
questions.solution: 5 total minus 3 shaded = 2 not shaded.

questions.question_text: Tom has two 5-dollar bills, three 2-dollar coins, and four 1-dollar coins. How much money does Tom have in total?
Circles and squares alternate in a row starting with a circle: O, S, O, S, O, S, ... What is the 10th shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='80' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10'/><rect x='160' y='10' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An alternating pattern of circle, square, circle, square continuing rightward.
questions.solution: Odd positions are circles, even positions are squares. The 10th position is even, so it is a square.

questions.question_text: Five friends sit in a row. Kim sits somewhere to the left of Leo, and Leo sits somewhere to the left of Mia. Which of these friends cannot possibly be in the rightmost seat?
questions.solution: Kim must be left of Leo and Leo left of Mia, so Kim has at least two people to her right and cannot be rightmost.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. If 3 slices are eaten, what fraction of the pizza remains?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='10' x2='50' y2='90'/><line x1='10' y1='50' x2='90' y2='50'/><line x1='22' y1='22' x2='78' y2='78'/><line x1='78' y1='22' x2='22' y2='78'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal pie slices by 4 lines through the center.
questions.solution: 8 - 3 = 5 slices remain out of 8, so 5/8 of the pizza is left.

questions.question_text: A fish tank holds 24 liters when full. It is currently 3/4 full. How many liters of water are in the tank?
questions.solution: 3/4 of 24 is (3 x 24) / 4 = 18 liters.

questions.question_text: Which of the four shapes shown has exactly 4 equal sides AND 4 right angles?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='50' height='50'/><rect x='80' y='20' width='60' height='40'/><polygon points='170,15 200,5 230,15 200,25'/><polygon points='170,70 200,40 230,70'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four shapes from left to right: a square, a rectangle that is not a square, a rhombus that is not a square, and a triangle.
questions.solution: Only the square has both 4 equal sides and 4 right angles.

questions.question_text: In a box there are red and blue balls. The number of red balls is twice the number of blue balls. Altogether there are 21 balls. How many red balls are in the box?
questions.solution: Let blue = b. Then red = 2b. b + 2b = 21, so b = 7 and red = 14.

questions.question_text: A square piece of paper is folded exactly in half twice to make a smaller square. If the final small square has area 9, what was the area of the original square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><rect x='90' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='120' y1='10' x2='120' y2='70'/><rect x='170' y='25' width='30' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three squares illustrating original, folded once, and final folded square.
questions.solution: Each fold halves the area, so two folds divide by 4. Original area = 9 x 4 = 36.

questions.question_text: Ben, Cara, Dan, and Eva each have a different favorite color among red, blue, green, and yellow. Ben's is not red or blue. Cara's is yellow. Eva's is blue. Dan's is not green. What is Ben's favorite color?
questions.solution: Cara = yellow and Eva = blue. Ben is not red or blue, so Ben is green or yellow; yellow is taken, so Ben = green.

questions.question_text: In the sequence 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, ..., each difference between consecutive terms is one more than the previous difference. What is the 8th term?
questions.solution: Differences: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. Terms: 1, 2, 4, 7, 11, 16, 22, 29. The 8th term is 29.

questions.question_text: How many rectangles of any size can be traced in the 2-row by 3-column grid shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='150' height='100'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='110'/><line x1='110' y1='10' x2='110' y2='110'/><line x1='10' y1='60' x2='160' y2='60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle divided into a 2-row by 3-column grid by internal lines.
questions.solution: Pairs of vertical lines chosen from 4 = 6. Pairs of horizontal lines chosen from 3 = 3. Total rectangles = 6 x 3 = 18.

questions.question_text: A clock gains 2 minutes every hour. If it is set correctly at 8:00 am, what time will the clock display at 2:00 pm on the same day?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='75' y2='50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An analog clock face with hour and minute hands.
questions.solution: From 8:00 am to 2:00 pm is 6 hours. The clock gains 2 x 6 = 12 minutes, so it shows 2:12 pm.

questions.question_text: Two different single digits A and B satisfy A + B = 9 and A x B = 18. If A < B, what is the value of A + 2B?
questions.solution: Since 3 + 6 = 9 and 3 x 6 = 18, we have A = 3 and B = 6. Then A + 2B = 3 + 12 = 15.

questions.question_text: Five cards numbered 1 through 5 are placed in five positions left to right. Position 1 holds card 2, position 2 holds card 5, position 4 holds card 1, and position 5 holds card 4. Where is card 3?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='55' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='145' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='190' y='10' width='40' height='40'/><text x='25' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2</text><text x='70' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>5</text><text x='115' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>?</text><text x='160' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>1</text><text x='205' y='35' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five card slots showing 2, 5, ?, 1, 4 from left to right.
questions.solution: Positions 1, 2, 4, 5 already contain cards 2, 5, 1, 4. Only position 3 remains for card 3.
